notes.body: assigned to @thutterer

1 Warning
:warning: This MR will trigger no release
## No release This Merge Request will trigger _no_ release. This means none of the commits warrant a semantic release according to [conventional commits](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-ui/-/blob/main/doc/contributing/commits.md) rules (e.g. they only update CI config). To trigger a release, update one or more commit messages. ## Reviewer roulette Changes that require review have been detected! A merge request is normally reviewed by both a reviewer and a maintainer in its primary category and by a maintainer in all other categories. To spread load more evenly across eligible reviewers, Danger has picked a candidate for each review slot. Feel free to [override these selections](https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/engineering/projects/#gitlab-ui) if you think someone else would be better-suited or use the [GitLab Review Workload Dashboard](https://gitlab-org.gitlab.io/gitlab-roulette/?currentProject=gitlab-ui) to find other available reviewers. To read more on how to use the reviewer roulette, please take a look at the [Engineering workflow](https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/engineering/workflow/#basics) and [code review guidelines](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/development/code_review.html). Please consider assigning a reviewer or maintainer who is a [domain expert](https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/engineering/projects/#gitlab-ui) in the area of the merge request. Once you've decided who will review this merge request, mention them as you normally would! Danger does not automatically notify them for you. | Category | Reviewer | Maintainer | | -------- | -------- | ---------- | | ~"frontend" | `@rchanila` [![profile link](https://gitlab-org.gitlab.io/gitlab-roulette/status-png/_user.png){width=12}](https://gitlab.com/rchanila) ![current availability](https://gitlab-org.gitlab.io/gitlab-roulette/status-png/rchanila.png){width=12} (UTC+10, 8 hours ahead of author) | `@pgascouvaillancourt` [![profile link](https://gitlab-org.gitlab.io/gitlab-roulette/status-png/_user.png){width=12}](https://gitlab.com/pgascouvaillancourt) ![current availability](https://gitlab-org.gitlab.io/gitlab-roulette/status-png/pgascouvaillancourt.png){width=12} (UTC-4, 6 hours behind author) | **If needed, you can retry the [🔁 `danger-review` job](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-ui/-/jobs/9687845604) that generated this comment.**

added 1 commit
  • aa344544 - chore(GlNav): Add controls for props to story
[Compare with previous version](/gitlab-org/gitlab-ui/-/merge_requests/5074/diffs?diff_id=1317639803&start_sha=113000b07842b3cf029be87de06a5c9521b26ae0)

This comment (and all props) are carried over from BNav. I don't really think we need this "in card" functionality, but I wanted to migrate it unopinionated. Otherwise this would be a breaking change, no :shrug:

changed this line in [version 3 of the diff](/gitlab-org/gitlab-ui/-/merge_requests/5074/diffs?diff_id=1321025062&start_sha=aa34454476fde2e6bf96cd6abf654b94f004f62a#ed593aaddbce14364921fe80f05fc78c7656e845_7_9)
I did not carry over this manual `inheritAttrs`/`$attrs`/`$listeners` handling. I don't see why it would be needed :thinking: Am I missing something?

From https://vuejs.org/guide/components/attrs#disabling-attribute-inheritance:

> If you do not want a component to automatically inherit attributes, you can set inheritAttrs: false in the component's options.

> The common scenario for disabling attribute inheritance is when attributes need to be applied to other elements besides the root node. By setting the inheritAttrs option to false, you can take full control over where the fallthrough attributes should be applied.

So it was done before so we can control which props and listeners we want on the gitlab-ui part of the component, and which ones on the Bootstrap Vue part.

Since we're removing the Bootstrap vue dependency here, `inheritAttrs: false` can be removed, and the props passed that are not defined in `props` will be applied on `<component>`. So indeed, it's valid to remove `$attrs` and `$listeners` if I'm not mistaken :blush:

@lorenzvanherwaarden Hi Lorenz :wave: Could you please review this bootstrap-vue removal MR?

This one removes BNav from GlNav, but keeps all supported props, so I think (but please double-check!) this is a non-breaking change.

I haven't created any integration branches yet. We don't use GlNav in gitlab, only in design.gitlab.com, afaik. Do we have an (semi-)automated way to create integration branches there? Let me know if I should create one manually!

FYI, after this one gets merged, I'll create a follow-up MR to delete the actual BNav vendor code. Doing this in a seperate MR should keep the review of this one here simpler.

Thanks for the context here @thutterer! :slight_smile: 

I'll only be able to get to review this on Monday, is this okay for you? If not, you can re-assign.
No worries @lorenzvanherwaarden I'm about to :homer-disappear: into the weekend in a bit, too, so please take your time with the review! Have a nice weekend! :wave:

Great work @thutterer! I think it's a good idea to split the migration and the cleanup to keep the MR from growing very big. AFAICT it makes sense that this isn't a breaking change.

I tested the Nav on a local running instance of `design.gitlab.com` and it seems to be working fine. But, I guess it wouldn't hurt to create an integration branch if that's the only place it's used? I don't think it's automated like it is for `gitlab-org/gitlab`, but I guess you could run the job for the integration branch in this MR and then use the url that would be used to point `@gitlab/ui` to in the package.json for `gitlab-org/gitlab` instead for the `package.json` of `design.gitlab.com` and open a MR?

I left 2 suggestions. I'm also wondering about the docs: will you update the [`nav.md`](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-ui/-/blob/main/src/components/base/nav/nav.md) with content from the [`README.md`](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-ui/-/blob/main/src/vendor/bootstrap-vue/src/components/nav/README.md) when you remove the Bootstrap Vue code later on or should we do that now? I see `src/vendor/bootstrap-vue/src/components/tabs/tabs.js` is still using `BNav` as well, so it might not be possible to already remove BNav at this time? 

Back to you :ping_pong:

Thanks for your helpful review @lorenzvanherwaarden :thumbsup: 

> you could run the job for the integration branch in this MR and then use the url that would be used to point `@gitlab/ui` to in the package.json for `gitlab-org/gitlab` instead for the `package.json` of `design.gitlab.com` and open a MR?

That's exactly what I did now :slight_smile: Links in the description. And direct link to an example page: https://design-gitlab-com-gitlab-org-gitlab-services-f6beaf2fb7fd4cad76.gitlab.io/review-mr-4372/components/badge/code/

Seems to work just fine, but I leave that up to you to decide :wink: 

With that, I think this is ready for another round :ping_pong:
Nice, thanks! Didn't get to this today but will take a look tomorrow morning

Thanks both!

@thutterer nice work, looking good! Just a few minor comments for your consideration but overall looking really good :tada:
Ah great review @peterhegman :thankyou: I'm EOD now, will make updates tomorrow, but I basically agree with all your comments :smile:
@peterhegman I think this now ready for another round :ping_pong:
@thutterer thanks for the changes, this looks great! Merging :tada:
requested review from @lorenzvanherwaarden
These spec got copied from the BNav spec file. Only change is the component name really.
**praise:** thanks for making this way more understandable than in Bootstrap vue!
**note:** I don't think Bootstrap vue has a default value for this prop, but it also does not mark it as required, so I think what you're doing here makes sense :thumbsup:

**suggestion:** I've seen (and I've done myself) adding the Bootstrap vue descriptions (from the docs site) for each prop as a comment above the prop itself. This is used in the story. See the [props for GlBadge](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-ui/-/blob/main/src/components/base/badge/badge.vue#L27) e.g..

Here, I think we can take them from Bootstrap Vue Nav: https://bootstrap-vue.org/docs/components/nav/#comp-ref-b-nav-props
Great idea! Done in a446b71439ca5ad5e37e236402231e74be89660a.

**suggestion:** I think we should add a default value `false` for all these boolean that are not required, to be more explicit. We tend to do that for the gitlab-ui component. There might even be a guideline but I don't recall where I saw that.

requested review from @peterhegman

**suggestion (non-blocking):** I find the BSV specs to be quite repetitive and making assertions that are unrelated to the actual description of the spec. For example I don't know if we need to have `expect(wrapper.element.tagName).toBe('UL');` in every single spec, only the ones related to the `tag` prop.

changed this line in [version 5 of the diff](/gitlab-org/gitlab-ui/-/merge_requests/5074/diffs?diff_id=1323638647&start_sha=7d6d756846c2e8cf3f9fdbfc9b003c8003d5be85#86409401354809daaeb1ae530c73b4cf4cccfc30_38_30)

I agree. I just had stopped myself of making too many changes at first, making it harder for reviewers to digest the diff between the bootstrap specs and their copy here. But you are right and I'm happy you suggested this yourself! :smiley: Reading through just this file... there was sooo much repetition. I removed all of that now and every `it` now only tests what it claims to test. Also for some reason all functions were `async` but hadn't had a single `await` so that's gone now too :put_litter_in_its_place:
Nice, thanks much cleaner!
**nitpick:** we shouldn't need to put `wrapper.destroy()` in our specs

changed this line in [version 5 of the diff](/gitlab-org/gitlab-ui/-/merge_requests/5074/diffs?diff_id=1323638647&start_sha=7d6d756846c2e8cf3f9fdbfc9b003c8003d5be85#86409401354809daaeb1ae530c73b4cf4cccfc30_43_31)

changed this line in [version 5 of the diff](/gitlab-org/gitlab-ui/-/merge_requests/5074/diffs?diff_id=1323638647&start_sha=7d6d756846c2e8cf3f9fdbfc9b003c8003d5be85#86409401354809daaeb1ae530c73b4cf4cccfc30_56_40)
Absolutely :done:

**praise:** this is much easier to follow than the [BSV code](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-ui/-/blob/main/src/vendor/bootstrap-vue/src/components/nav/nav.js#L9), jeez that is confusing :laughing:

**issue:** I think not passing `v-on="$listeners"` could potentially be a breaking change since Vue doesn't automatically pass listeners to the root element. I'd probably suggest adding this back just to be on the safe side. WDYT? 

`v-bind="$attrs"` should be done automatically for us since we removed `inheritAttrs: false`
Thanks for raising this! I added it back now :this-is-fine:
